extraction.one_line_summary: GROVES Kaden won a chaos-marred sprint after crashes at 7km and inside final 300m eliminated key rivals on rain-soaked roads.
tactical_patterns: PINOT Thibaut targeted KOM points early from break then soft-pedaled back, revealing points jersey farming tactic over stage commitment.
ZOCCARATO Samuele solo attack from breakaway within 25km shows late aggression ineffective when peloton controls at sub-40kph average speed.
Alpecin, Trek-Segafredo, DSM all committed leadout trains despite treacherous conditions, none willing to cede organizational advantage despite crash risk.
Crashes at 7km forced ROGLIC Primoz and GC riders to chase hard, exposing vulnerability window before 3km safety rule applies.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: crashed twice (dog-related incident mid-race, again inside 3km), showed fragility in chaotic conditions despite escaping time loss
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: back wheel slipped on white line in final sprint, crashed at finish line suggesting bike handling issues in wet
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: positive: navigated two major crash points (7km, final 300m) to win maiden Giro stage, shows superior wet-weather positioning skill
rider_signals.MILAN Jonathan: positive: second place plus intermediate sprint points while defending maglia ciclamino demonstrates consistency in treacherous sprint finishes
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: caught in 7km crash but successfully chased back, showing recovery capacity under pressure
rider_signals.LEKNESSUND Andreas: positive: retained maglia rosa through crash chaos at 7km, DSM team control effective despite conditions
